title: Gas report - Week 40
summary:
LNG
The LNG spot market has been subdued, With rates falling, it would seem to be the result of the same old stories; requirements are low with few tenders/cargoes offered, greater than expected ship availability, gas storages are well above previous levels - the list goes on. But we are fast approaching the main fixing window of winter, with cargoes now being shown for end November and coming into December some brokers believe that there will be changes afoot. Rates themselves were softening as Aus-Japan BLNG1g fell $22,461 week-on-week to close at $163,608 while out in the Atlantic both BLNG2g and BLNG3g lost just shy of $30,000 to finish at $140,635 and $170,035, respectively, for Houston-UKC and Houston-Japan.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: The LPG market has seen quite a turnaround of late. Within a few weeks of the highest rates ever recorded on the Baltic LPG Index, we have seen a further $42.286 overall fall on BLPG1 Ras Tanura-Chiba.
